successor: Harte-Hanks (later Aberdeen acquired by The Aberdeen Group / Spiceworks Ziff Davis)
notes: Incorporated 1988; entered market 1990; co-founded by Kastner, Casale, Logan, Willmott. Name scored well in focus-group research as trusted but unplaceable.
Co-founder of Aberdeen Group; covered system software, application software, and x86 servers. Recruited from DEC by Casale.
Co-founder of Aberdeen Group. Previously ran Investor Relations at Prime Computer. Held registered financial analyst credential. Covered scientific and high-performance computing.
Co-founder of Aberdeen Group. Harvard MBA. Previously published Kastner’s Yankee Group study on transaction processing. Covered minicomputers. Participated in the 1997 VC decision meeting.
Co-founder of Aberdeen Group. Came from IDC. Covered networking and remaining white space. Proposed the 1997 venture capital initiative; Kastner notes departure risk was a factor in accepting it.
First analyst hire by Kastner at Aberdeen. Previously worked with Kastner’s wife. Deep expertise in database systems, data management, query optimization, lock-escalation patterns.
First analyst hire by Kastner at Aberdeen alongside Kernochan. Previously worked with Kastner’s wife. Covered enterprise applications and business intelligence.
Wall Street Journal technology journalist; press partner referenced in chapter context.
Boston Globe technology journalist; press partner referenced in chapter context.
Founded 1979. Dominant firm in FIGY; subscription model targeting CIOs with vendor evaluations. Creator of the Magic Quadrant framework.
Founded 1964. Dominated market sizing and forecasting; owned quantitative shipment/revenue/market-share data. Tom Willmott came from IDC.
Founded 1983 by George Colony. Carved out business technology strategy territory; known for Wave reports and rigorous methodology.
Founded 1970. Focused on telecommunications and networking. Required reading in late 1980s as networking became enterprise-critical. Logan previously published Kastner’s Yankee Group study.
Founder of Forrester Research (1983).
Founder of Gartner Group (1979). Referenced implicitly through Gartner Group founding year context.
Casale ran Investor Relations at Prime Computer during its rocket-ship years; stock climbed 272% in a single year. Kastner and Casale were colleagues there.
Kastner was at DEC when Casale called him in late 1989 with the Aberdeen proposition.
Press partner; Bill Bulkeley was a technology journalist there.
Press partner; Hiawatha Bray was a technology journalist there.
Kastner hired a Linux analyst as soon as Red Hat took off, reflecting Aberdeen’s open-architecture conviction.
